reasoning: Strong baseline performance with clear bottlenecks: Q3 hash join is critical (74ms gap), Q6 SIMD is significant (12ms gap). With 1 iteration remaining, focusing on high-ROI optimizations (priorities 1-3) targeting these bottlenecks should close the 97% gap with DuckDB to 21-46%. Expected combined improvement: 36-54ms (140ms → 86-104ms).
focus_areas: Q3 linear probing hash table with prefetching (20-30ms expected gain)
Q3 compact group key layout (10-15ms expected gain)
Q6 full SIMD pipeline for filters and aggregation (6-9ms expected gain)
strategy_notes: Prioritize Q3 hash table optimization as it addresses the critical bottleneck (74ms gap). Implement both hash table (P1) and compact keys (P2) together since they're complementary and low-risk. Q6 full SIMD (P3) is independent and can be implemented in parallel. Avoid P4 (Q1 SIMD) due to small absolute gain (3-6ms) for medium risk. Skip P6 (data loading) as it doesn't impact execution_time target.
notes: For Q3 hash table: use power-of-2 sizing for fast modulo, implement prefetching 8 rows ahead. For compact keys: use __attribute__((packed)) and verify no alignment issues. For Q6 SIMD: extend beyond date filtering to cover all predicates and revenue computation, use selection vectors to avoid wasted work. This is the penultimate iteration - be thorough but conservative.
